methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Thunder Tiger operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company, having raised $22M+ through 2025 under founder and current CEO Gene Su. The valuation universe comprises 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Peer-median valuation was not applicable to this analysis. The Defense & Security sector contains 20 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow peer set, falling short of the minimum threshold required for reliable median benchmarking. An all-sector median approach would introduce material noise given the sector-specific dynamics of defense and security valuations.

Without sufficient narrow-set comparables and absent triangulated results from alternative methodologies in the provided dataset, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. Key uncertainties include the precision of comparable company financials across the limited public set, the relevance of private peer funding rounds to current market conditions, and stage-specific risk factors in the transition to public markets. Further data collection on profitability metrics, revenue multiples, or precedent transactions within the Defense & Security space would be necessary to establish defensible valuation support.
